A New Life in the Sun

programme.title: Dirty Rotten Scoundrels
programme.desc: The south of France. Two rival con men -- one is suave and sophisticated, the other is a third-rate American hustler -- make a wager.

programme.title: The Negotiator
programme.desc: The tables turn when a seasoned hostage negotiator takes hostages and risks all to prove his own innocence.

programme.title: In Time
programme.desc: Justin Timberlake and Amanda Seyfried co-star in this sci-fi thriller set in a future world where aging stops at 25, and the only way for one man to stay alive is to steal more time.

programme.title: Assassin's Creed
programme.desc: A man discovers he is a descendant of an ancient line of Assassins.

programme.title: The Double
programme.desc: An edge-of-your-seat espionage thriller, starring Richard Gere and Topher Grace as ex-CIA and FBI agents, trying to find the killer of a US senator, which bears the hallmark of an infamous Soviet assassin, thought to be dead.

programme.title: Hot Pursuit
programme.desc: In this riotous spin on the mismatched buddy movie, an uptight and by-the-book cop (Reese Witherspoon) tries to protect the gregarious widow (Sofía Vergara) of a drug boss as they race through Texas pursued by crooked cops and murderous gunmen!

programme.title: Dirty Work
programme.desc: A spiteful loser finds his true calling when he opens a highly successful business specializing in revenge.

programme.title: Wildlife
programme.desc: A teenage boy must deal with his mother's complicated response after his father temporarily abandons them to take a menial and dangerous job.

programme.title: The Internship
programme.desc: Old-school salesmen Billy (Vince Vaughn) and Nick (Owen Wilson) try to reboot their careers by joining Google’s fiercely competitive internship program — with hilarious results!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Pranks for Nothing / Zombie Dearest
programme.desc: Eager to regain his lost prank mojo, McGee releases a mischievous pixy named Hanky Pranky.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Diagnosis: Deletion
programme.desc: Blue Angel’s looking to Speed Duel her way to victory, but the diabolical doctor she’s battling is set on prescribing a dose of defeat!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Golfadoom / There is Something About Mamba
programme.desc: McGee, Gretchen and Buttsquat must beat Haggis MacJockstrap in golf!
programme.sub-title: Golfadoom / There is Something About Mamba

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: True Blue
programme.desc: Blue Angel hopes to give her fans something to scream about by defeating Playmaker, but after receiving a dangerous card from the Knights of Hanoi, her fans might be screaming for all the wrong reasons!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Cluck of the Were-Chicken / Gnome Force
programme.desc: When a hulking were-chicken stalks the camp, McGee must lead the hunt to prove his innocence.
programme.sub-title: Cluck of the Were-Chicken / Gnome Force

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: A Bridge Too Far
programme.desc: Spectre stoops to a new low when he reveals a game changer that leaves Playmaker without a play to make!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Buttastic Journey / Monkey See, Monkey Kung Fu
programme.desc: McGee is shrunk down to the size of an atom!
programme.sub-title: Buttastic Journey / Monkey See, Monkey Kung Fu

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: The opening day race for the new A-R-C racing season is threatened when the villainous Black Cloud - under the leadership of General Raven - kidnaps Maggie Strong, daughter of league chairman George Strong.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Sweat, Hot Lakebottom Summer / The S'Mogre
programme.desc: A heat wave forces McGee to sneak a dab of Sawyer's anti-sweat cream. After trying to cheer up Armand, McGee creates a s'more so big, it topples and coats him in delicious goo.
programme.sub-title: Sweat, Hot Lakebottom Summer / The S'Mogre

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Desperate
programme.desc: The kids try to gather the three Requiem Blaster minicons together. Sky Blast at first resists, then sings the others to him to save Optimus. They all realise there's more to the minicons than first appears.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: To Gore Or Not To Gore
programme.desc: Can Gore turn his duel around before the doctor of DNA deletes him!
programme.sub-title: To Gore Or Not To Gore

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Decisive Battle
programme.desc: Confident of his ability to finally overcome the Autobots, Megatron challenges Optimus to battle. However, when all seems lost Optimus' minicon summons the Skyboom Shield to protect his master.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Story Time!
programme.desc: Ai updates Roboppi’s processors on the who, what, where, and why of LINK VRAINS!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Bloody Marty / Ghost In The Mower
programme.desc: The kids ignore Rosebuds warning and summon Bloody Marty from the ominous mirror world.
programme.sub-title: Bloody Marty / Ghost In The Mower

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Ring Around The Gretchen / Chilli Con Carnage
programme.desc: Beauty pageant Gretchen looks to connect with her girly side.
programme.sub-title: Ring Around The Gretchen / Chilli Con Carnage

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: The team heads for the Siberian Tundra for a race that involves using ski-attachments on the jets.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: The Brave Battler
programme.desc: With LINK VRAINS under attack, it’s up to the flashiest duelist ever to save the day – Shima!

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Seize the Wind!
programme.desc: The Knights of Hanoi have set their sights on stealing Playmaker’s AI – but he won’t give it up without a fight!

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Chase
programme.desc: Trapped in cyberspace with Sideways, Alexis, Rad, Carlos and their minicons become aware of a massive evil entity, far more powerful and dangerous than Megatron, lurking threateningly in the background. Or was it all a dream?

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: When Master Mayhem tries to upload a cool new viral video, he totally #fails, and now everybody on planet Earth is laughing at him! So he’s sending Teensy the Tri-Terror-Tops to pulverize our planet, and delete the internet.

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: Master Mayhem has caught a serious case of Space Flu! This overlord’s gonna take a sick day from planet-conquering, so he’s sending Dome Diddy Dome, powered by a hot dog inside its dome, to dish out some super sick destruction on Earth.

programme.title: Street Sharks
programme.desc: The Street Sharks' geneticist father, DR. BOLTON, discovers his jealous but brilliant colleague, DR. PARADIGM, is using Bolton's recombinant DNA discoveries to create a new, more powerful species.

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: After playing the “home version” of one of his favorite competitive eating shows, Chow receives a phone call naming him winner of a cruise for him and all his friends. Edgar will be staying home.

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: In a desperate attempt to get the crystals back from the KFDP, Skor SHRINKS down his hapless brother Skrap and sends him to infiltrate the KFDP headquarters. As Skrap is taking off with a crystal, a very tired Chow accidentally eats him!

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Their previous trip to Bograth having been interrupted, the kids return to the swamp realm to search for its Dreamstone. They encounter a herder named Gorran dipping a large cage containing Bograthian Creatures into an oozing black puddle.

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Deviating from the quest to fulfill a mission from Orwin, the Final Dreamers find themselves escorting an old nemesis to Naroom: Shalax the bounty hunter. The Shadow Magi want him too!

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: During a race at the floating Neptune Islands, the team encounters a squadron of old bomber jets that disappear in front of their eyes. The local urban legend says they are a ghostly squadron of jets that are trying to find their way home.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Pain and Gain
programme.desc: The Gore - LINK VRAINS’ biggest and boldest bruiser - is looking to pin Playmaker for the three count!

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: The Deleted
programme.desc: Duelists are mysteriously being logged into LINK VRAINS against their will, and it’s up to Yusaku to find out why!

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: The Reveal
programme.desc: Varis reveals his true identity – as well as the terrifying truth behind the Ignis!

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Trust
programme.desc: The Autobots and Decepticons face off amongst snowy mountains in the battle for the latest minicon. Despite using the Star Sabre, this time Megatron loses - and Scavenger finally gains the Autobots' trust.

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: Master Mayhem is packing for a little vacation! He found a super cheap hotel, and while he’s away, the Earth will pay! He’s sending Macho Cheese to crumble Earth’s Champion and cover our planet in cheese… and cheesy jokes!

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Prehistory
programme.desc: The children learn more about the history of the minicons and the war. The latest minicon alert leads the team to a volcanic island: the volcano erupts in the middle of the usual 'Bot vs 'Con battle.

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: When the dinos discover a bunch of animals run amok all over Megalopolis, they wonder why these animals have fled from the woods and entered the city. Kane figures that Skor must have something to do with it.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: The Lost Incident
programme.desc: The chilling details of the past are finally revealed as Playmaker continues his battle with Zaizen.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Duh'Rehka / Chore Leave
programme.desc: While chasing his gopher-nemesis, Sawyer gets zapped into his brain bin and emerges super smart!

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: On the eve of a solar eclipse, General Raven travels to the great Pyramids of Egypt, to locate the artifact that will trigger an ancient Egyptian solar weapon capable of taking out any target.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Mars
programme.desc: Alexis tracks a minicon in the vicinity of Mars. Starscream gets there first, but is disabled by Tidalwave. Jetfire rescues him: on the way back Starscream spots and tries to entice the minicon. But first they must get past Megatron.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: An electrifying race through the famed ‘Thundercloud Alley’ causes the jet of one of Trey’s greatest rivals to suddenly gain sentience, and turn on it’s pilot.

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: The World Poker Tour storms into Los Angeles as six poker pros play like they're double parked in the action-filled final table of the Bicycle Casino's "Legends of Poker" championship tournament.

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: The World Poker Tour hosts the Aruba Poker Classic for an action packed bracket style battle involving some of the game's top poker professionals.

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: Poker gets wild in the Central American paradise of Costa Rica as six players - two American and four international - vie for hundreds of thousands of dollars in prize money.

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: Talk about a Rush! The next stop on the World Poker Tour season is the largest poker tournament in northern California history, as the WPT and Lucky Chances Casino host the Gold Rush Tournament with its half a million dollar prize pool.

programme.title: WPT Classics
programme.desc: Foxwoods, the world's largest casino needs every inch of room to hold the talent at this week's World Poker Tour final table.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 1
programme.desc: Jay banishes Ant's backside buddy, and Alice helps Chelsea get over the shock of her life.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 2
programme.desc: Sketch slays Fiona's zombie, while Glen gives magician George a frightening new friend.

programme.title: Jersey Shore: Family Vacation: Pork Roll or Taylor Ham?
programme.desc: The roommates come up with an elaborate scheme to kidnap Deena and bring her to the house, while newly single Ronnie takes time to himself to come to terms with his breakup.

programme.title: Jersey Shore: Family Vacation: Chicken Cutlets and Ketchup
programme.desc: Vinny's mom, Paola, prepares a grand Italian feast for Mike, and Uncle Nino tags along. Meanwhile, Angelina invites an unexpected guest out to the bar.

programme.title: Jersey Shore: Family Vacation: The Shorefather
programme.desc: It's premiere night for the guidos as they screen a movie they made in Mike's honor. Then, they cram eight months into one night when they re-enact all the moments Mike missed while he was away in prison.

programme.title: Jersey Shore: Family Vacation: Unresolved Issues
programme.desc: It's bachelorette party time as the girls head to New Orleans to celebrate Angelina. But unresolved issues threaten to blow the whole thing up.

programme.title: Ex on the Beach: Mad Frank
programme.desc: Ex on the Beach will see eight single guys and girls head to an idyllic beach to enjoy a summer holiday in paradise. That is until all of their ex's show up!

programme.title: Ex on the Beach: Don't Hate the Player
programme.desc: The fourth Ex arrives at the beach. Ross feels Chloe's wrath as his relationship with Vicky advances, Talitha and Ash hit the rocks. Marco and Frankie reach breaking point over Emily.

programme.title: Ex on the Beach: Playing with Fire
programme.desc: The fifth Ex arrives at the beach to surprise Vicky and Ross, things look increasingly bad for Talitha and there's a shock departure which leaves everyone panicking over whose Ex is next?

programme.title: Ex on the Beach: Tuletha's Got a Secret
programme.desc: Ash is shocked by the arrival of the sixth Ex at the beach. Emily and Liam go on the date from hell and Talitha is rocked as a secret threatens to ruin her relationship with Ash forever.

programme.title: Ex on the Beach: The Truth's Out
programme.desc: Ross tells Chloe that he'd like to give things another go. Jack tries it on with Emily, Farah gets possessive over Joss. Ash's world changes when Joss reveals his secret kiss with Talitha.

programme.title: Ex on the Beach: The Champion of Exes
programme.desc: Ash and Talitha split and he wastes no time in hooking up with Chloe. Shelby and Ross get closer. Vicky has the ex arrival that she's been dreading as it all kicks off on the beach.

programme.title: Laguna Beach: The Bonfire
programme.desc: The love triangle between Stephen, Kristin, and LC is becoming more obvious, and each of them is really starting to feel the pressure.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 4
programme.desc: Lisa pays tribute to her idol Robbie Williams, while Jack covers his literal leg tattoo.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 3
programme.desc: Sketch covers Chris's boastful nickname, and Glen removes Darren's chat up line leg art.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 4
programme.desc: Lisa pays tribute to her idol Robbie Williams, while Jack covers his literal leg tattoo.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 5
programme.desc: Sketch conceals a large portrait on Les. Alice spreads Becca's favourite food on her leg.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 6
programme.desc: Glen tackles Hannah's four-letter faux pas, and Jay gives firefighter Simon a hot design.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 7
programme.desc: Sketch tackles Nick's measuring stick, while Glen helps heathen Bob get an honour inking.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 8
programme.desc: Jay tackles Mark's Greek tragedy, while Alice helps Lois to celebrate her penguin persona.

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 196
programme.desc: Should you really never bring a knife to a gunfight? Will you lose a sword fight if you try to strike first? Did the 14th-century Chinese design a two-stage, arrow-launching missile?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 202
programme.desc: Can certain sound frequencies persuade people that an area is haunted? Is it really possible to smell fear? Is it really as easy to move and bury a dead body as it is in the movies?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 129
programme.desc: Is pykrete tougher than ice, bulletproof and durable enough to make a boat out of? Can a V-shaped snowplough really split a car in two?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 104
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a person with no flight training land a jet while being assisted by the traffic controllers over the radio? Is it possible to talk while falling at terminal velocity?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 87
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a human voice extinguish a flame? Can hypnotists plant suggestions in unwilling patients? Can hypnosis help bring back dormant memories?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 45
programme.desc: Myths tested: Do steel toe boots really put toes in more danger than a regular boot, to the extent that the steel toe would chop off the toes? Can bottle rockets be used to launch a human?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 151
programme.desc: Are some types of people more tolerant to pain? Can a BBQ propane tank heat up enough in a fire to launch through a garage roof like a rocket?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 186
programme.desc: Can a pallet of duct tape help you survive on a deserted island?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 227
programme.desc: Could Luke have safely swung across the chasm in the Death Star while carrying Princess Leia? Would the Ewoks' swinging log trap have destroyed an Imperial walker?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 179
programme.desc: Will a super-sized Newton's cradle work? Can the weight of a bird be enough to tip a teetering car off of a cliff?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 162
programme.desc: Can a car's tire pressure affect its fuel efficiency? Can a laptop stop a bullet from point-blank range? Can a hair weave stop a .40 caliber bullet? Can a fridge door stop a round of 9mm bullets?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 205
programme.desc: Can you cook a full holiday meal on the engine of a running car? Does the tryptophan in turkey make you sleepy? Is it easy to confuse the taste of unusual meat with chicken?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 139
programme.desc: Does a car explode when driven off a cliff? Can a huge rocket launch a cage holding a human, and would the person survive?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 228
programme.desc: Can an explosion caused by a hot water heater put out a fire? Is sunscreen flammable? Will a vacuum explode if it sucks up black powder?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 56
programme.desc: Myths tested: Did Archimedes make a cannon using steam power? Is a cardboard cereal box more nutritious than the sugary cereal inside?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 96
programme.desc: Myths tested: Is it possible to climb a building with a motorized grappling hook? Will getting punched by someone wearing a ring leave a ring-shaped imprint?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 1
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a person be propelled off a toilet seat by dropping a lit cigarette into it when filled with combustible materials? Is running better than walking to keep dry in the rain?

programme.title: Black Knight
programme.desc: When a wisecracking black hustler from South Central L.A. finds himself transported back to medieval England, he leads the overthrow of an evil king and wins the heart of a proto feminist woman.

programme.title: Trees Lounge
programme.desc: A man drifting through time desperately searches for some meaning to his life.

programme.title: Homegrown
programme.desc: When the leader of their dope-growing operation gets rubbed out, three inept underlings try to take over. Starring Billy Bob Thornton, Ryan Phillippe, Jamie Lee Curtis and John Lithgow.

programme.title: Ghost World
programme.desc: The story of neo-cool Enid and Rebecca who, faced with graduation from high school take a hard look at the world they wryly observe. When Rebecca focuses her attention on mutual romantic fixation Josh, the girls friendship is forever changed.

programme.title: Bring Me The Head of Mavis Davis
programme.desc: Music producer Marty discovers that the woman he turned into a household name, Mavis Davis is losing her fame. The only way to secure her fame is for her to die. The problem is, she refuses to die.

programme.title: Big Mommas: Like Father, Like Son
programme.desc: Malcolm Turner and his stepson Trent go undercover at an all-girls performing arts school

programme.title: Memoirs Of An Invisible Man
programme.desc: When a government experiment goes awry it renders an innocent bystander invisible.

programme.title: Superhero Movie
programme.desc: An orphaned teenager is bitten by a radioactive dragonfly, granting him superhuman powers in this parody of films like Spider-Man, X-Men, and Fantastic Four.

programme.title: Moonstruck
programme.desc: In her Academy Award winning role, Cher, while waiting for her timid fiance to decide between her and his mother, falls in love with her fiance's estranged brother, a bakery worker with a love of opera and life.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Pink Rider': Pinky starts a delivery service and is menaced by a motorcycle gang and a monster truck company. 'The Midnight Ride of Pink Revere': When Paul Revere is conked on the head by a horseshoe, Pinky has to take over the famous ride.
programme.sub-title: Pinky Rider/The Midnight Ride Of Pink Revere

programme.title: Count Duckula: Hardluck Hotel
programme.desc: Duckula goes to stay at a worn out and run down hotel for the weekend.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Mobile Home
programme.desc: Thinking he is rebuliding the castle, Duckula accidentally sells it to The Crow Brothers in disguise, brick by brick!

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: Bill and Ted accidentally break Missy's brand new antique vase. The phone booth is set for ancient China to find a replacement. After a brief stint as prisoners who are ordered to build a wall, they serve as unlikely deckhands for Marco Polo.
programme.sub-title: One Sweet And Sour Chinese Adventure To Go

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Due to a fluke in the narrator's microphone, Danger Mouse and Penfold are transported to the year 1215, where they have to act out characters from Robin Hood.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Prime-Time Duck
programme.desc: Transylvanian Television arrive at the castle to feature it in a television programme.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Historic buildings such as Tower of London, Eiffel Tower, Kremlin and the Empire State Building have disappeared.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback has placed a large number of sand pyramids all over England, threatening to sink the island Danger Mouse travels to the Sahara Desert to stop him.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: A special breed of metal eating bugs developed by the military is stolen by VENOM.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: VENOM steals a load of plutonium from the Army to create a nuclear water pump machine which can pump water from lakes into fault lines and cause earthquakes.

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Zombie Awakes
programme.desc: Dr Quackbrain lures Duckula to his dream machine for his greatest experiment.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Dr. Goosewing & Mr Duck
programme.desc: Goosewing creates a formula where when drunk, the victim becomes the complete opposite of their original selves.

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: Reel Pink
programme.desc: Pink steps into the movies when Big Nose tries to evict him from the theater.

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: Bill and Ted go in search of Babe Ruth so they can replace their coach's autographed baseball. On their trip back in time, they stop to teach the rules of baseball to the Yankee and Confederate soldiers at Bull Run.
programme.sub-title: This Babe Ruth "Babe" Is A Dude, Dude!

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Baron Greenback uses a shrink ray to enter Colonel K's body to learn all of the Colonel's secrets. Danger Mouse and Penfold follow, but they get diverted because Greenback changed some of the signs. They also have to deal with the body guarding cells.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Home Stretch Pinky': Horse trainer Pinky tries to help Granny win the Kentucky Fedora - the world's famous horse race. 'Pink Pucks': To win a big hockey game, Pink conjures up Slusho to play goalie, only to have him lured away by an ice seductress.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold track Greenback down to a New York City skyscraper run by International Oddbods and a guy who wears a bag over his head. Inside, there are all sorts of bizarre things going on.

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: Pink Suds and Clean Duds
programme.desc: Pink goes to the laundromat, but Big Nose wants all of the machines for himself.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse must stop Greenback who has placed a device at the North Pole to stop the Earth's rotation and cancel gravity.

programme.title: Count Duckula: O.O. Duck
programme.desc: The Egg decides to steal Castle Duckula to take over the world.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Restoration Comedy
programme.desc: Bored with the look of the castle, Duckula hires a designer to redecorate the castle.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'The Magnificent Pink One': When a town needs a new Sheriff, Pinky gets the job, but the baddie Brothers are coming to town. 'Downhill Panther': Pinky competes against a ski resort owner to decide who gets control of the mountain they both occupy.

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: Enchanted Pinkdom
programme.desc: Little Pink Riding Hood travels through a fairly tale land to get a basket of goodies to his godmother, but he must stay away from the Big Nose Bad Wolf.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback captures Colonel K and has the Colonel send Danger Mouse and Penfold on holiday to a haunted castle in Transylvania.

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: Cleanliness is Next to Pinkliness
programme.desc: Big Nose and Pink Panther have neighboring showers, but only enough hot water for one of them.

programme.title: Count Duckula: No Sax Please - We'r'e Egyptian
programme.desc: Count Duckula, Igor and Nanny travel to ancient Egypt in an attempt to recover an ancient artifact.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold puts on Danger Mouse's suit and cannot get out, which confuses Colonel K into thinking he is Danger Mouse. Later, DM and Penfold go to Bratislavakia to find out why all the countries of Central Europe have swapped places.
programme.sub-title: There's a Penfold in my Suit

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback kidnaps Penfold to lure Danger Mouse into a spaceship and send both of them to a planet ruled by old fashioned looking machines.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The time-travelling grandfather clock makes its return as it appears at Windsor Castle.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: VENOM steals a shrinking ray from the inventor and uses it to steal rare cars from an exhibit.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: hile cutting trees in the forest, Jacques discovers a petrified (stone) tree.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Bombay Duck
programme.desc: Duckula gets tricked by a fake Genie into going to India.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The Earth is flooded with instant custard. Danger Mouse and Penfold travel to outer space to find the Custard Mite of Glut, a small creature that can eat up the custard, but the planet that houses the Mite is controlled by armoured aliens.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: Clutch Hawks enters Wildcat in the demolition duel in Memphis, only to learn Vanessa Warfield and Bruno "Mad Dog" Sheppard are also entered, with Manta and Stinger.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: Pink, Pink and Away': Super Pink flies into action when the Dogfather robs a bank and then accidentally kidnaps a little boy. 'Down on the Ant Farm': The Panther's experimental ant farm, complete with Ant, is accidentally delivered to the Aardvark.
programme.sub-title: Pink, Pink And Away/Down On The Ant Farm

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Stool Parrot': The Inspector and Panther must protect a witness to a fifty million diamond robbery...the Parrot. 'Pinky and Slusho': When the Panther's sno-cone business is threatened by Manly Man, the Panther creates Slusho, a talking snowman.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Mystery Cruise
programme.desc: Duckula is sat alone in the castle waiting for Nanny's favourite TV show "Super duper double drachma deal" to start.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: VENOM follows ancient clues carved into the mountains of South America that will lead them to a treasure.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse discovers that Baron Greenback is behind a wave of attack robots that have been going after agents that work with DM.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Duck and the Broccoli Stalk
programme.desc: Duckula, Igor and Nanny find themselves up a broccoli-stalk and into a giant's castle.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Under orders from a supercomputer, all the world's machines mutiny and refuse to do their jobs.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold gets a pop-up book of monsters from a mysterious book sales club, but it turns out to be from J.J. Quark who turns the monsters in the book alive. Danger Mouse eventually turns the tide by drawing in a monster of his own.
programme.sub-title: Have You Fled from any Good Books Lately?

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Space alien J. J. Quark announces that he is going to claim the Earth as his.

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: Make Pink, Not War
programme.desc: Pink Panther is caught in a crossfire between two feuding Big Noses with water balloons.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold travel to Australia where they pick up top secret plans from an agent rat in the form of a rugby ball. Penfold gets distracted by a boomerang and is later picked up by large bird of prey.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy view a four-bedroom house in Tyne and Wear, a detached bungalow in Rochester and a house in Plymouth.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 20
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a three bedroom semi in Stoke, a property in Plymouth and a flat in central London.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy view a semi in County Durham, a garage in Devon and a 1930s flat in South London.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit properties in Essex, Devon and Croydon.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 10
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a three bed semi in Nottinghamshire, in Reading there's a tiny plot in a peaceful location and not all it seems to be in a three bedroomed house in Cardiff.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 4
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts see a semi-detached property in Ilford in Essex; a three bedroom, ex-miner's cottage in the Welsh town of Cwmparc in the Rhondda Valley; and an old Blacksmith's shop in the beautiful Dorset village of Puncknowle.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 13
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a 4 floor property in Southampton, a house in Hartlepool in need of an electrician and a top floor flat in London.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 11
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a mid terraced house in Stoke-on-Trent, an old health spa in Kent and revisit an old Chapel in Nottinghamshire over two years since we first visited it.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 3
programme.desc: Lucy & Martin travel to Folkestone to see a Victorian house that could offer someone a foot on the property ladder. Then it's on to Hertfordshire to see a 3 bed terrace and in Devon there's an old inn up for sale where last orders have been called.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 20
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a house in Barnet, a property in Derbyshire that has undergone some huge changes and a plot of land in Wiltshire.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 9
programme.desc: Lucy and Martin visit a three bedroom house in Essex with unusual features, a roomy flat in Battersea, South London, and an end of terrace house in Liverpool. We see what happens when they go under the hammer!

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Lucy Alexander look at a cottage in Cheshire and a flat in Stoke.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 27
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a bungalow in Lincolnshire, a maisonette in Buckinghamshire and a three story house in Nottingham.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 11
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a pleasantly surprising terrace in Cheshire, a large flat in London with great views and a house in Torquay.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 19
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts visit a forgotten building in Lancashire, a 1930s house in Derbyshire and a workshop in Devon.

programme.title: Erik The Viking
programme.desc: Erik the Viking and his men travel across the sea to find Valhalla to ask the gods to end the Age of Ragnarok.

programme.title: La Grande Bouffe
programme.desc: A group of men go to a villa in the French countryside where they resolve to eat themselves to death.

programme.title: Scream, Blacula, Scream!
programme.desc: Brought back to the world of the living by a voodoo ceremony Blacula (William Marshall) is loosed with insatiable bloodlust upon the population of a small community in the Louisiana back country. Pam Grier also stars in this scintillating sequel

programme.title: Hackers
programme.desc: A neophyte hacker becomes the target of the FBI after he unknowingly taps into a high-tech embezzling scheme which could cause a horrific environmental disaster in this suspenseful drama.

programme.title: Vampire Hookers
programme.desc: Carradine is an aged vampire who has a bevy of vampiric beauties who lure many of their customers back to his lair. A pair of virile young Navy sailors get mixed up in their shenanigans.

programme.title: The Cartier Affair
programme.desc: Curt Taylor is severely indebted to the top dog at California State Prison. After being released from jail, Curt takes a job as Cartier Rand's secretary to steal her jewels and settle his debt. Starring: Joan Collins, David Hasselhoff, Ed Lauter

programme.title: A Night In Casablanca
programme.desc: The Marx Brothers are employed at a hotel in post-war Casablanca, where a ring of Nazis are trying to recover a cache of stolen treasure. (1946) Starring: Groucho Marx, Harpo Marx, Chico Marx

programme.title: Blacula
programme.desc: The eternally cool William Marshall puts a fresh spin on the age-old legend of the vampire, condemned to wander the Earth with an insatiable lust for blood in "Blacula," often credited as the first African-American horror film.

programme.title: La Dolce Vita
programme.desc: A series of stories following a week in the life of a philandering tabloid journalist living in Rome.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Women are more often prey than predators, but the exceptions are deadly women indeed. In this episode, we’ll discover what drove these deadly women to prey on the unsuspecting.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Greed is one of the oldest motives for murder, and these deadly women are no exception: they’ll do anything to satisfy their need for cash. This is the dangerous world of the fortune hunters.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Greed
programme.desc: Known as “black widows,” these women kill their spouses, lovers and children for profit. Murder for them becomes a business. Their trade is murder. Their profit? Blood money.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Crimes of revenge are usually single acts of passion. A brew of hatred and restitution boils over in a manic moment of fury. Once caught, revenge killers usually display remorse and atonement.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: The path towards revenge can sometimes lead to murder. It can be quick, fiery and violent. It can also be a dish served cold, the product of a calm, calculated plot.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Sometimes, when a man and a woman unite, a terrible urge for destruction can awaken in them both. Happy couples become deadly teams, intent on one goal: murder.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: While marriage is meant to be for life, it often ends in divorce. These wives took their marriages vow seriously: "till death do us part."

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Blood ties are strong ties, sometimes strong enough to pull a person into a murderous conspiracy. When a family member wields his or her power, the result can be death.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: While their sisters chose a life of sensible, suburban motherhood, these deadly women chose a life of crime. They lived with the thrills, the danger and the deadly consequences of being outlaws.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Married to Murder
programme.desc: To the outside world, their marriages seemed happy. But behind closed doors, there was no domestic bliss. These women wanted out, but getting a divorce wasn’t their preferred option.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: We want to believe that all women would give their lives to protect their children. But there are horrifying cases when a mother will turn against her natural instinct and kill her own flesh and blood.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: These women would stop at nothing to get their hands on the cash they needed. From husbands to strangers to other people’s babies, they spilt blood for money.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: The mind of a female killer is shaped by a complex cocktail of influences. Do people become that way, driven by horrendous events in their lives? Or are some killers born bad?

programme.title: Deadly Women: Love to Death
programme.desc: Love and money are a beautiful marriage, for the lucky ones they create a life of happiness and ease. Unless the love is of money, the bride is a killer, and the groom their victim.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Psychopathic killers lurk in society, mostly acting alone. Sometimes fate brings them into contact with another person who shares their cold power to kill.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Secrets and Lies
programme.desc: Behind their friendly smiles, deadly women hide histories of bloodshed and murder.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Obsession
programme.desc: Deciphering what causes minds to corkscrew out of control is the complex work of the criminal profiler. Obsessions can take many forms: beauty, devotion, ego and power.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Nurse Beverley Allitt overdosed multiple infants, Stella Nickell poisoned medicine capsules in stores and Kathleen Follbigg killed four of her own children.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Without Remorse
programme.desc: A small town without any crime was turned inside out by the arrival of one dangerous stranger. Some killers are cold and calculating; others are impulsive, unpredictable, and hard to pin down.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Dishonored
programme.desc: Shirley Russell was determined enough to reach the rank of Captain in the United States Marine Corps. When this officer turned up missing, her colleagues refused to believe she had gone AWOL. It was unclear whether Captain Shirley Russell was alive.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Killing Zone
programme.desc: In the 1990's the war on drugs was in full swing. The front lines were often America's inner cities where gangs use violence and murder to rule their empires.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Crime Spree
programme.desc: They swept through six states like a plague, thrill killers acting without rhyme or reason. They targeted adults and young girls, kidnapping, and murdering. And in their rampage, they left plenty of clues, but moving targets are the hardest to hit.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The World Trade Center Bombing
programme.desc: Terrorist bombings are what happen in other countries. Not here in the United States. And yet, in 1993, it happened in New York City.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Human Prey
programme.desc: Thomas Dillon killed five outdoorsmen at random over a period of three years in rural southern Ohio. After a tip led the FBI to the murder weapon, ballistic testing made a match.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The C-11 Squad
programme.desc: New York City is not stranger to gang violence. But when a 12-year-old child was kidnapped, the gangs had crossed a line. Law enforcement raced to find the missing boy before it was too late.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A daring kidnapping and a bizarre ransom note baffled Mississippi authorities. The victim was the wife of a wealthy businessman. A list of 12 names and a few drops of blood were the only clues to her fate.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Business
programme.desc: Many escape the poverty of India to start new lives in the United States. One turned the American dream into a nightmare. People started turning up dead in New York's Indian community-authorities struggled to link fraud and murder to a powerful man.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Blood Brothers
programme.desc: When the small community of Noel, Missouri discovered their bank was robbed, they're shocked. When the bank president turned up missing, they're outraged. And when clues began to surface the facts were more terrible than anyone could have guessed.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Murdering Cowboy
programme.desc: The sun set on the Wild West long ago, but some people refused to notice. Claude Dallas was one of them. A wily trapper, Dallas typified the rugged individualism that forged this country. He went too far when he killed two game wardens in 1981.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Predator
programme.desc: In the early 1990's, Washington, D.C., became the murder capital of the nation. Few were left untouched by gang violence, which was ignited by the drug trade. By 1994, that violence would strike at the very heart of law enforcement.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Dead Run
programme.desc: In Pennsylvania, an inmate escaped with the help of his female accomplice. Following a trail of fraudulent credit card receipts and deaths, authorities track the pair, but always find themselves one step behind.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Stranger
programme.desc: The body of a young woman found burned in a truck launched a manhunt to find her killer. When more bodies began to turn up across the country, the FBI believed the cases were linked. Investigators had a suspect. They just couldn't find him.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Small Town Terror
programme.desc: In a small Ohio community, an elderly woman and a teenaged boy disappeared. Because they vanished within hours of each other, police believed the cases were linked. But they didn't know how.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The True Story of Mississippi Burning
programme.desc: Philadelphia, Mississippi, 1964. A sleepy southern town steeped in tradition finds itself targeted for invasion.
programme.sub-title: The True Story of Mississippi Burning

programme.title: The FBI Files: Death in Alaska
programme.desc: The day began like any other for Cheryl and Paul Chapman, until the silence of a peaceful Sunday morning was shattered with a phone call.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Lord Chitterly, an English traitor who looks like the bumbling Colonel Crittendon, enters Stalag 13 with information for the Germans. Crittendon is made up to replace the Lord and fool the Germans.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Lord Chitterly, an English traitor who looks like the bumbling Colonel Crittendon, enters Stalag 13 with information for the Germans. Crittendon is made up to replace the Lord and fool the Germans. The real Lord escapes.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: The heroes try to prevent the Gestapo from sending Klink and Schultz to the Russian front.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Schultz is now the temporary Kommandant of Stalag 13 and starts to abuse his new power.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan and company tell a German mole, disguised as an American aviator, all about their operation.
programme.sub-title: Eight O'Clock And All Is Well

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Col. Hogan and his Heroes assist local resistance forces sabotage hidden explosive stashes the Gestapo plans to use to destroy bridges and other resources as the Nazis prepare to retreat from advancing Allied Forces.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Tiger, the pretty French underground fighter, is going to be executed in Berlin and Hogan's Heroes are to the rescue.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Radio detection equipment prohibits Hogan from using their usual radio so they borrow the one in Klink's car.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: LeBeau is passed off as a Gypsy fortune teller to help get a radar countermeasure device to London.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan has to help three German scientists escape from Stalag 13 and get to London.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Colonel Hogan and Commandant Klink go to London to steal a plane as part of a plan to ferret out German spies in England.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Posing as German officers, the Heroes try to get a German spy to disclose with whom he was working.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Disguised as a German officer's wife, Newkirk collects penicillin for the underground.
programme.sub-title: That's No Lady, That's My Spy

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: To capture some secret papers, Hogan has a Russian woman convince Klink to volunteer for the Russian front.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan helps prove the German officer that Klink is defending is innocent of treason so he can get the plans that he had stolen for the Allies.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: The Heroes have to figure how to get a new type of rocket back to England.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan must get a U.S. general out of camp just as General Burkhalter's sister shows up with her intended - Klink's new number two.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan has to discredit a German officer who knows about the operations at Stalag 13.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: The underground team that needs help in blowing up a mobile rocket-launcher battery includes a very lovely woman.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: The prisoners create an elaborate underground ruse to fool a German spy that has been placed in their ranks.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Learning that the German army is manufacturing an improved Tiger tank, Hogan and his men are determined to steal one, so they can disassemble it and make blueprints for the Allies.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan concocts a Kommandant of the Year award to distract Klink so a new Nazi rocket being stored protectively at Stalag 13 can be sabotaged.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.desc: Homemade gules to test stickiness; puzzle with a scientific optical illusion; supersonic car.

programme.desc: The science of genetic modification in Hawaii; shattering a glass; curing a dust allergy through eating worms.

programme.desc: A demo of calorific value by taking part in the Oxbridge Boat Race; testing MP's abilities to calculate risk.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: Liz investigates sunburns; making museum visitors act younger and smarter.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: The science of IVF; following couples who undertake IVF treatment; going 360 degrees on a swing.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: MEGAFLOODS
programme.desc: Water covers two thirds of the earth’s surface, and we are utterly dependent on it, but when this vital ingredient of life unleashes its fury on the planet it becomes the ultimate bringer of death.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: CONSTRUCTION
programme.desc: Bridges and tunnels have transformed the way we live, making the inaccessible easy to reach, and turning time consuming journeys into convenient commutes, but when things go wrong they can go very wrong indeed.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: AIR, LAND AND SEA
programme.desc: Planes, trains and ships, our mass transit systems, have had a profound effect on mankind, making the world smaller and more connected.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: HURRICANES
programme.desc: Hurricanes, Typhoons and Cyclones all the same thing with different names – what they also have in common is their destructive power! Hurricanes are large, swirling storms and winds from a hurricane can damage buildings or trees.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: TORNADO
programme.desc: A tornado is a violently rotating column of air extending from the base of a thunderstorm down to the ground. Tornadoes are capable of completely destroying well-made structures, uprooting trees, and hurling objects through the air like deadly missiles.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: VOLCANOES
programme.desc: Volcanoes are Earth’s geologic architects. They’ve created more than 80 percent of our planet’s surface and their explosive force has crafted both mountains and craters.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: MANMADE
programme.desc: When it comes to disasters, mother nature has mankind beat. However, as mankind expands, evolves and technology advances, it comes with dangerous challenges. When errors are made or unforeseen circumstances occur, the results can be devastating.

programme.title: Moon Shots: Inside the Lost Apollo Archive
programme.desc: The world famous Apollo missions are some of the greatest feats ever accomplished by man. With rare archive footage and unprecedented access to life on board the Apollo spacecrafts, this stunning one-hour special brings scientific history to life.

programme.title: Neil Armstrong: One Small Step
programme.desc: Neil Armstrong has stamped his place in history by becoming the first person to walk on the moon.

programme.title: The Apollo Experience: Apollo 17: Part 1
programme.desc: Between 1960 and 1972, mankind boldly went where no human had been before. Across 11 manned missions, 24 men flew to the moon and 12 walked upon its surface. This film documents Apollo 17 through the eyes of the astronauts who flew.

programme.title: The Apollo Experience: Apollo 17: Part 2
programme.desc: In this episode, hear first hand testimonials and dialogue from the incredible men who walked on the moon. Incredible footage and photos give a unique and beautiful experience of their data collection process and journey home.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: What Is Space?
programme.desc: Brian Greene reveals space as a dynamic fabric that can stretch, twist, warp, and ripple under the influence of gravity, and explores dark energy, which makes up 70% of the universe.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: The Illusion of Time
programme.desc: Brian Greene takes us on the ultimate time traveling adventure, hurtling 50 years into the future before stepping into a wormhole to travel back to Big Bang.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Join Brian Greene on a wild ride into the weird realm of quantum physics, which governs the universe on the tiniest of scales.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: Universe or Multiverse?
programme.desc: Cutting-edge theories are suggesting that our universe may not be the only universe. Instead, it may be just one of an infinite number of worlds that make up the multiverse.

programme.title: The New Frontier: From There To Here
programme.desc: Series introduction and the study of Earth from orbit.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Return To The Moon
programme.desc: The Moon, our closest celestial companion. Our indispensible dance partner through the cosmos.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Inner Worlds

programme.title: The New Frontier: Next Stop Mars

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Planet Makers

programme.desc: It powers the machinery of nature; our weather, encourages and sustains life on land and at sea.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Exo Planeta
programme.desc: As scientists and astronomers peer closer at nearby stars with ever more fidelity they are discovering planets; exo planets, worlds outside our own solar system.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Edge of The Universe
programme.desc: The night sky is a time machine. The further we look out into the universe the further back in time we reach.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Astronauts

programme.title: The New Frontier: Getting There

programme.title: The New Frontier: Orbit

programme.desc: Our solar system is vast, from our own star the Sun to Earth is nearly 150 million kilometers or 1 Astronomical Unit.

programme.title: The New Frontier: First Light

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown

programme.title: The New Detectives: A Federal Offense
programme.desc: When there's a difficult case to crack  whether it involves drugs, arson, or weapons  the investigators and scientists of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco and Firearms have the means to crack it.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Mind Hunters
programme.desc: Psychological Profiling: Journey into the dark recesses and calculated madness present only in our worst nightmares, and in the minds of serial killers.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Lies and deceit can often throw investigators off the trail of justice. But when hard evidence contradicts a killer's story, police must use the clues to piece together the truth.

programme.title: The New Detectives: For Love Or Money
programme.desc: Every family has its secrets, and sometimes blood relations lead to bloodshed. When money is the motive, murder can rip at the very foundation of marriage and family.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Bloodlust

programme.title: The New Detectives: Short Fuse
programme.desc: Explosives Investigations: The crime lab is the place where science meets murder. In New York State, Eleanor Fowler opened a small package, which as mailed to her home. When she lifted the lid the box exploded killing her instantly.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Murder For Hire
programme.desc: For some killers, murder can be a profitable business. And the scene of the crime can be both a source for clues, and puzzling questions. When a victim has been targeted for death, investigators must look beyond the obvious to uncover a murder for hire.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Tainted Blood
programme.desc: The forces of nature can reduce a body to bones in a matter of weeks. Using a unique combination of art and science, forensic anthropologists give victims a face long after they have been forgotten.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Remnant of Blame
programme.desc: The solution to the most heinous crimes often hinge on the smallest of clues. Investigators must have their eyes trained to find the full story of a murder written in a single scrap of evidence.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Deadly intentions
programme.desc: When abduction turns to murder, forensic science is the only key to finding justice for the victims of a kidnapper's deadly intentions.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Random Targets
programme.desc: Most victims of multiple murderers are meticulously chosen because of a mutual connection with the killer or because they match an intricate set of criteria that fits the killer's MO.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Stranger Than Fiction
programme.desc: A millionaire is found dead, murdered for a stash of buried silver. A young woman dates violent men, only to be killed by her best friend.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Burning Evidence
programme.desc: Identifying Burned Remains: It's difficult to have a murder investigation without a body, and burning up the victim is a time-honored method of destroying physical evidence.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Unlikely Sources
programme.desc: Some of the best clues come from the least likely places. Baffling crimes have been solved and criminals betrayed through evidence provided by insects, beer bottles, and other seemingly meaningless objects.

programme.title: The New Detectives: A Deadly Smile
programme.desc: Investigators rely on forensic odontology to identify a body from a single tooth and to catch two brutal killers from their bite marks.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Out To Kill

programme.desc: 54 year old Simon Buis was savagely beaten and kicked to death in an Auckland park in 1980 by persons unknown. To this day, his killers have evaded capture.

programme.desc: In 2006, 19-year-old Blake Stott died in a mysterious car fire. Blake had seemingly pulled into a South Otago lay-by to have a sleep, when his late-model, un-modified car burst into flames.

programme.title: Sensing Murder

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Taken Out
programme.desc: Was Donovan Reidy deliberately run down and left to die? In 1995, 23-year-old Donovan Reidy was at a party when he got into a fight. A short while later, his bloodied body was found on the side of a major New Zealand highway.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: In Too Deep
programme.desc: In 1999, Kiwi engineer Aaron Hopa and his English colleague Robert Glazzard were murdered on board a survey ship in the Persian Gulf in.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Fallen Angel
programme.desc: In this episode of Sensing Murder we learn about the murder of Angela Blackmoore, a 21 year old woman with a 2 year old child. The child was not harmed and the killer has not been found.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Into Thin Air
programme.desc: Jim Donnelly was a hard-working family man living a quiet life in suburban Auckland. For 20 years he’d worked as a scientist at the Glenbrook Steel Mill, south of Auckland. On the 21st of June 2004, he simply vanished into thin air.

programme.title: Sensing Murder
programme.desc: Our new team of “ Sensing Murder” psychics are challenged to unmask the killer – or killers – of vivacious New Zealand hairdresser Paula Brown who was plucked from Sydney’s downtown after a night out with friends.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Stone Cold
programme.desc: In 2004, Brendan Percy was found dead of hypothermia and dehydration in a sub alpine area above Oamaru in suspicious circumstances.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: A Bump in the Night
programme.desc: In 1980, six-year-old Alicia O'Reilly was raped and murdered in her bed, while her sister slept less than a metre away, and her mother sleeping across the hallway.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Out of Aces
programme.desc: Garth Doull trained to be a policeman but the lure of the race track saw him swap sides of the law to become a consummate con-man. On the 8th of September 1989, his body was found floating in Wellington harbour.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Lost Soul
programme.desc: In 1985, 25-year-old Luana 'La Verne' Williams disappeared from her Tauranga home. Eight years later her disappearance was upgraged to a murder enquiry. Her body has never been found.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Walk of Innocence
programme.desc: In 1970, eighteen-year-old Olive Waiker disappeared as she walked across Rotorua to babysit her nieces and nephews. Her semi-naked body was found hours later in a rest area south of the city..

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Now You See Me
programme.desc: 26-year-old deaf woman Alexa Cullen vanished from her home in Manaia, on the Coromandel Peninsular in 1995. She had a history of hitch-hiking and police suspected she’d run away. But three years later, ominous rumors began circulating in the community.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Sensing Murder Insights - Special
programme.desc: Police detectives from around New Zealand reveal exciting new developments sparked by the Sensing Murder psychics in Series 1.
