programme.title: COPS starts at 9pm
programme.desc: Cops gives viewers access into the daily lives and work of the men and women of law enforcement. Filmed on location, the groundbreaking series follows real officers on patrol as they conduct high-speed pursuits, wild arrests and dangerous police business.

programme.title: COPS starts at 9pm
programme.desc: Cops gives viewers access into the daily lives and work of the men and women of law enforcement. Filmed on location, the groundbreaking series follows real officers on patrol as they conduct high-speed pursuits, wild arrests and dangerous police business.

programme.title: COPS starts at 9pm
programme.desc: Cops gives viewers access into the daily lives and work of the men and women of law enforcement. Filmed on location, the groundbreaking series follows real officers on patrol as they conduct high-speed pursuits, wild arrests and dangerous police business.

programme.title: COPS: I Always pay my Debts
programme.desc: A man tries to bite an officer after he was spotted with narcotics. Officers respond to a family fighting in their front yard. A man claims he was just paying someone back when he tried to buy drugs.

programme.title: COPS: Skipping Rocks
programme.desc: A man who ran from police complains that he was not read his rights. Officers detain kids who threw rocks at windowfronts. A paranoid man claims he is being chased by drug dealers.

programme.title: COPS: Tow Time
programme.desc: Police conduct a high-speed chase on a car with a missing tire. Police respond to a tow truck driver being assaulted. Police are called when a relative attacks a woman's husband.

programme.title: COPS: Borderlands
programme.desc: Officers attempt to detain undocumented border crossers. Border patrol discovers a driver with a load of drugs in his car. Officers check passports and a suspicious-looking laptop.

programme.title: COPS: Visiting Hours Twofer
programme.desc: Police respond to a report of prostitution in a hospital parking lot. A man and woman engaged in solicitation are pulled over. A woman in an area known for prostitution tries to evade police.

programme.title: COPS: Ignorance is an Excuse
programme.desc: Two men engage in a fist fight on the street over an alleged robbery. A couple claim that they didn't know the car they are driving was stolen.

programme.title: COPS: Checked Luggage
programme.desc: Agents of the US Customs and Border Protection pursue a vehicle. Border agents spot two undocumented people. Airport Customs detains a man when agents suspect he might be carrying more than luggage.

programme.title: COPS: Uncomfortable Shoes
programme.desc: A driver takes police on a neighborhood chase. Officers are called to capture a 12-foot python hiding outside a home. Officers suspect a motorist with a funny walk is hiding something up his shoe.

programme.title: COPS: In-Law Problems
programme.desc: US Marshals are tasked with finding a young boy that has been kidnapped for ransom. A man suspected of dealing contraband is turned in by his ex-girlfriends mother.

programme.title: COPS: Set Some Boundaries
programme.desc: A couple gets into a fight with their in-laws when they attempt to take their baby on a 3am drive. A man is attacked by his roommate after he gets 'too friendly' with a woman in the home.

programme.title: COPS: Invisible Intruders
programme.desc: A homeowner shoots at intruders only he can see. The driver of a stolen truck claims he's just trying to return the vehicle.. A argument about parenting styles turns ugly when a gun is pulled.

programme.title: COPS: Dance Fighting
programme.desc: A dispute inside a nightclub turns into a street fight. A man patrols his neighborhood 'Old West' style. A K-9 unit is called in to track a burglar on the run.

programme.title: COPS: Do Not Disturb
programme.desc: A prostitute tries to outrun the police during a foot chase. A man claims he can only meet women at motels since he lives with his mom.

programme.title: COPS: Tree Hugging
programme.desc: An ex-felon threatens to kill his spouse after she announces she want to divorce him. A driver has suspicious plants in her car. A woman crashes her friends truck into a pedestrian and a tree.

programme.title: COPS: Crawling to Vegas
programme.desc: A driver leads police on a slow speed chase towards Las Vegas. Three people are suspected of using contraband. A multi-neighbor dispute devolves into a fight involving guns and bats.

programme.title: COPS: First Do No Harm
programme.desc: A suicidal man tries to taunt police into shooting him. Undercover officers bust up an illegal operation at a pain management clinic. A woman claims she's just a bystander to a motorcycle hit and run.

programme.title: COPS: Safe Cracking
programme.desc: A man stages a sit-in in his car in an attempt to stop it from being towed. A homeowner denies that he's selling drugs despite the large amount of evidence found in his home.

programme.title: COPS: High Jump Fail
programme.desc: A family with kids is taken on a high speed car chase. An intoxicated suspect tries to jump a fence while protesting that he's not trying to run away.

programme.title: COPS: Bumming a Smoke
programme.desc: A runaway motorcycle tries to elude officers by driving on a rough road. An argument over a cigarette turns ugly. Kids throw rocks from an overpass at the wrong car.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Quiz Show
programme.desc: Sabrina realizes the importance of her Quizmaster after she inadvertently gets him fired.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Fear Strikes Up A Conversation
programme.desc: Public speaking seems like a breeze for Sabrina after coming face-to-face with her fears in the Other Realm.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Rumor Mill
programme.desc: Gossip flies when Sabrina does witch community service in the Other Realm rumor mill.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: When Teens Collide
programme.desc: When Sabrina switches personalities with Libby, she uses her magic and newly found popularity.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Mom vs. Magic (Season Finale)
programme.desc: Sabrina faces the most difficult decision of her life when she must choose between her mom and her magic

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Disney World
programme.desc: A school trip to Disneyworld becomes yet another test for Sabrina on the road to her witch's license

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina’s Choice
programme.desc: After her aunts decide to go their separate ways, Sabrina has to choose which of the two should raise her.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Boy Was My Face Red
programme.desc: An embarrassing moment leaves Sabrina red in the face.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: It’s a Mad Mad Mad Mad (Season Premiere)
programme.desc: Sabrina learns she has to solve the family secret before she can use her Witches License.
programme.sub-title: It’s a Mad Mad Mad Mad (Season Premiere)

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Suspicious Minds
programme.desc: Jealousy gets the best of Sabrina when Harvey and Libby are paired together for a school project.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Pom-Pom Incident
programme.desc: Sabrina's good intentions go bad when she prevents Valerie from trying out for the cheerleading squad.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Pancake Madness
programme.desc: An addiction to pancakes threatens to send Sabrina’s life spinning out of control.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Good Will Haunting
programme.desc: Halloween turns truly frightful when a demented doll takes charge of the festivities.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: You Bet Your Family
programme.desc: Salem’s gambling debts get the best of him, but its Sabrina and her aunts who pay the price.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: And The Sabrina Goes To
programme.desc: A spell to get her just desserts leaves Sabrina with egg on her face instead.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Nobody Nose * Libby Like Sabrina Knows * Libby
programme.desc: An out-of-control magic spaceship lands Sabrina and Salem inside Libby’s brain.
programme.sub-title: Nobody Nose * Libby Like Sabrina Knows * Libby

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Christmas Amnesia
programme.desc: It’s beginning to look a lot like Christmas until Sabrina accidentally erases the holiday

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina and The Beast
programme.desc: In order to teach Sabrina a lesson about love and beauty, Harvey is turned into a beast.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Whose So-Called Life is it Anyway?
programme.desc: Sabrina’s charitable magic turns out to be anything but a good deed.
programme.sub-title: Whose So-Called Life is it Anyway?

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina The Matchmaker
programme.desc: It’s Valentine’s Day, and Sabrina must help two people fall in love or suffer Cupid’s wrath.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: What Price Harvey?
programme.desc: Magical ambition shows its dark side when Harvey drops out of school to become an industrial tycoon.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina And The Pirates
programme.desc: Purchasing a fake ID in the Other Realm causes Sabrina’s magic to become fake as well.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Mrs. Kraft
programme.desc: Mr. Kraft’s ex-wife returns to win him back, but Zelda isn’t going to let him go without a fight.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina The Teenage Writer
programme.desc: When typed on a magical typewriter, Sabrina’s spy story and its characters come to life.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Salem The Boy
programme.desc: Salem's return to human form is short-lived, thanks to Roland the Troll and Sabrina's unknowing wishes.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Long and Winding Short Cut
programme.desc: Sabrina is stripped of modern conveniences after using shortcuts to solve her family secret.
programme.sub-title: The Long and Winding Short Cut

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina The Sandman
programme.desc: Employed as a Sandman, Sabrina gets into trouble dropping into other people’s dreams.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina’s Pen Pal
programme.desc: Sabrina discovers her new pen pal witch is wanted in two realms.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Good The Bad And The Luau (Season Finale)
programme.desc: Sabrina and her aunts travel to Hawaii for a family reunion, but when they arrive, Sabrina discovers that she will not be able to enjoy herself until she solves the family secret once and for all.
programme.sub-title: The Good The Bad And The Luau (Season Finale)

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Silent Movie
programme.desc: The Spellman house becomes the setting for a silent movie.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina’s Real World
programme.desc: Sabrina becomes the unknowing star of an Other Realm TV show.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Big Sleep
programme.desc: A vengeful aunt uses magic poppies to put the Spellman household to sleep.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Dream A Little Dreama Me
programme.desc: Her Witches License and a new student bring Sabrina a new set of challenges.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: No Place Like Home (Season Premiere)
programme.desc: Sabrina’s senior year of high school gets off to a rocky start.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Salem And Juliette
programme.desc: Sabrina helps Salem find a date for his high school reunion.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Jealousy

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Phantom Menace
programme.desc: It’s Halloween, and Sabrina is having a ghoulish time adjusting to her new job at the coffee house.

programme.title: Total Drama Island
programme.desc: A surprise awaits reigning champs Owen and Gwen.

programme.title: Total Drama Action
programme.desc: The new contestants arrive to compete against each other in movie-themed challenges for a variety of rewards. Who will win the first one and get the pick of the trailers?

programme.title: Total Drama Action
programme.desc: The cast think they've been set an easy challenge to find hidden alien eggs around the set - that is until Mama Alien starts hunting them down.

programme.title: Total Drama Action
programme.desc: The cast is put into two teams, Gwen's team, the Screaming Gaffers and Trent's, the Killer Grips. Which team will succeed in today's gruelling challenges?

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's last jar of chocolate spread has been pinched by the Lemmings, who are savouring it in secret.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Wedged in his sofa to relieve his aching back, Grizzly baulks at getting up. He designs clever strategies to do everything from a distance, including confiscating the Lemming's radio-controlled toy robot transformer, which he will use as a butler.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The vibrations created by Grizzly's snoring during his nap give the Lemmings an idea for a bouncing game. But when Grizzly changes positions and his snoring stops, the Lemmings must find other sources of vibrations.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a birthday piñata in the ranger's living room. The idea of bursting open a papier-mâché statue with a bat as it says in the instructions pleases him.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Inside a mysterious cave, Grizzy and the Lemmings discover a magical Game of the Goose. The events displayed in the squares that the players land on come true in real life.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is bothered by an object stuck in between the sofa cushions. It's a big fur lemming puppet glove and the Lemmings believe that the puppet is a Super Lemming who's stronger than Grizzy and they start to obey it.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly discovers the leftovers of a Halloween party in the ranger's cabin, which was entirely redecorated for the occasion.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: When the last jar of chocolate spread they were fighting over sinks to the bottom of the river, Grizzy and the Lemmings have no choice but to dive in to recover it. But to their great surprise, they discover you can't breathe in water.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a strange figurine with the power to switch the spirits of two living beings and finds himself imprisoned in the body of a caribou.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: While salmon fishing on the riverbank, Grizzly hauls in an unusual catch: an old hand mirror straight out of a fairytale; instead of showing the reflection of the person who holds it, the mirror shows an idealized vision of his surroundings.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: In a casing that fell from a circus caravan, the Lemmings find a magic stick that can enchant objects and make them come alive.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly calls upon his fishing instincts to seduce the she-bear, but only succeeds in making a fool of himself. When he sees the Lemmings riding a magnificent wild salmon like rodeo cowboys, he tries to steal theirs.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Nutty Hill is suffering from a stifling heat wave. Searching in vain for a bit of cool air, Grizzy suddenly has a brilliant idea.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Just as Grizzly was about to coat a nice big fresh salmon with chocolate spread and snuggle up in front of the TV, he discovers that the pantry is empty.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's nap is interrupted by an annoying vibration, the forest ranger's cell phone. It has various functions, including a game to which he instantly becomes addicted.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Freed by the thawing ice, a mysterious giant egg slides up to the front door of the log cabin and the Lemmings watch with interest as it hatches.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy decides to slim down with the ranger's bodybuilding equipment, but before starting his exercise routine, he will first have to settle the problem of the Lemmings, who are already using the machines as an amusement park.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: By chance, Grizzly discovers that the ranger's new robot vacuum cleaner also gives very pleasant back massages.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy discovers a gadget that controls various functions in the cabin. He will finally be able to get rid of the Lemmings, without even leaving the couch.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: To make sure that the Lemmings don't snatch his chocolate spread, Grizzy turns the fridge into a safe. However, the Lemmings see Grizzy open the door while sleepwalking.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have invented a new popcorn game which consists of throwing popcorn and catching it with their mouths.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly wants to learn to dance the mambo to please his lovely she-bear. He finds a learning kit at the ranger's home which has a dance carpet with footprints.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: After receiving a terrible blow on the head, Grizzly thinks he's a rodent and tries to get the gang to adopt him.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is exhausted, and he goes to fetch a pillow and a sleeping mask, but the Lemmings have entirely different plans for the couch.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy discovers that the frenzied breakdance the Lemmings are doing is actually due to the fleas they are infested with.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Another heat wave, and even the riverbed is dried up. One very thirsty Grizzly discovers that the Lemmings have used the last remaining bottles of water to fill their inflatable pool.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have caught a little spider. Her sticky threads are perfect for bungee jumping. Grizzly, who has no intention of getting caught in the web, decides to crush the insect.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings draw inspiration from lessons they find in a martial arts book and start breaking every wooden object they can get their hands on into two. When Grizzy tries to stop them, they go on to the next lesson and get him in a judo hold.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy dreams that he's a valiant knight who has delivered his beloved. As he is about to take her in his arms, he is woken by the Lemmings who have chosen this day to blast music.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy stands in front of the pantry, desperate. The stock of chocolate spread is completely depleted. He realises that the Lemmings are making some themselves, following a complicated recipe.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Inspired by a magazine with a jewellery ad showing a man offering a diamond to a woman, Grizzly imagines himself offering the ranger's engagement ring to his beloved she-bear.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: When he accidentally sticks a computer microprocessor to his temple, Grizzy is transformed into an exceptionally intelligent being capable of predicting events to come far in advance.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings prepare a dream afternoon snack with the last jar of chocolate spread in the cupboard. Tough luck, Grizzy is coming. Caught off guard, the Lemmings hide the jar in a teddy bear-pyjama case to slip away without arousing suspicion.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy gleefully discovers that he can print images taken with the ranger's camera as it's connected to a state-of-the-art printer. He decides to frame himself, but when the time comes to print his portrait, there's no more paper in the printer.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: A van pulls up to the ranger's cabin to deliver a full crate of jars of chocolate spread. While Grizzy and the Lemmings fight over a wrecking bar to open it, someone steals the crate.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: As usual, Grizzly picks the door of the Ranger's log cabin to come in and make himself comfortable, but this time it's impossible to enter. The Lemmings have requisitioned the cabin for a party and barricaded themselves inside.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have got their hands on some white beans which have very strange properties, when mixed with soda anyone who eats them turns into a bouncing ball. Grizzy realises that if he wants to have a nap, he must get rid of the magic beans.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly wants to get rid of the old piano in the garage at any cost, for the Lemmings love using it to make a dissonant racket. But when he notices a she-bear he's in love with likes the melody of Fur Elise, he decides to learn the piece.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Still in hot pursuit of our heroes, Team Rocket is ready to make yet another attempt to catch Pikachu—until disaster strikes! It soon becomes apparent that Meowth is getting sick!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Putting her idea of catching Jigglypuff aside for now, Misty promises to find a solution to this Pokémon’s singing situation!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: According to Ash’s rival Gary, fossils from extinct Pokémon have been discovered in the area, triggering a great fossil rush in the canyon. Ash decides to join in the excavation as a chance to beat Gary!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Ash is sleeping peacefully until he is awoken by Brock and Misty with alarming news: there’s something wrong with Pikachu’s voice! When Ash becomes concerned, he runs to the nearest Pokémon Center with Pikachu.

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: As our heroes are walking in the countryside, they notice a 'Missing Persons' poster of an undeniably familiar face: it looks exactly like James from Team Rocket! Is it really him in the poster?

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Our heroes discuss the rumor that a rare wild Farfetch has been seen in the area. Ash and Misty both vow to catch it, and soon they get their chance when the Pokémon appears!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: On leaving the Pokémon Center, our heroes discuss what they think will hatch from the Pokémon Egg Ash discovered. Nearby, Team Rocket begins hatching another daring plan to make it theirs!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: During a battle with another Trainer’s Rhyhorn, the bulb on Bulbasaur’s back begins to glow, prompting Ash to take it to the local Pokémon Center. They soon discover that Bulbasaur isn’t sick—it’s getting ready to evolve!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: On their way to Cinnabar Island, our heroes join Officer Jenny and the police force’s Growlithe in some special training, hoping to improve Pikachu’s battling skills.
programme.sub-title: The Case of the K-9 Caper!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: On a brief stop for lunch, Ash, and his friends meet Todd Snap, a Pokémon photographer who has been trying to get a perfect shot of Pikachu.

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Realizing that he hasn’t won any Gym badges for a while, Ash signs up for the Pokémon League Admissions Exam, a great chance to prove he’s got what it takes to become a Pokémon Master!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Ash and the gang decide to check out a breeding center that they saw on a commercial. However, the center is a cover for a diabolical scheme from Team Rocket members Butch and Cassidy—old rivals of Jessie and James!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Misty and Jessie compete to be Queen of the Princess Festival! Ash and Brock lend Misty their Pokémon for the battle, and Jessie takes a licking despite a surprise Pokémon of her own.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: It’s Kids Day, and Ash and friends help some schoolchildren celebrate. Team Rocket spoils the fun by grabbing a kid instead of Pikachu. In a shocking turn of events, Meowth becomes a good guy…or does he?

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Our heroes travel to Cinnabar Island, where Ash hopes to win the Volcano Badge. A strange character offers riddles about how to find the Gym, where Ash and Pikachu have a fiery battle with a Pokémon they’ve never seen before!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash has finally located the Gym, suspended above a pool of molten lava! This battle for the Volcano Badge can only be won by a strong Fire-type Pokémon, so Ash must rely on his misbehaving Charizard.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: While Ash and the gang hurry to catch the last ferry off Cinnabar Island, they get sidetracked by a group of Squirtle, Wartortle, and Blastoise that need assistance!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Misty's Horsea desperately needs exercise, so the gang decides to take it to her sisters' Gym in Cerulean City. There, she learns that her sisters are planning an exciting underwater ballet.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash, Brock, and Misty get sidetracked on their way to Viridian City when all of their belongings mysteriously disappear. While searching for their missing things, they find themselves mixed up in a close encounter that’s really out of this world!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Our heroes make their way to Viridian City, where Ash hopes to earn the Earth Badge. They get to the Gym just in time to see his rival, Gary, being defeated by an unknown Pokémon—and Gary insists that it's evil!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: On their way back to Pallet Town, our heroes encounter a runaway Mr. Mime. Ash disguises himself as another Mr. Mime so he can get close. When Team Rocket strikes, will they catch the right Mr. Mime?

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Our heroes find themselves at a fork in the road and decide to climb a steep and dangerous mountain. Pikachu gets lost, and they must all work together to find it, stay warm, and weather a storm.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: On a visit to Professor Oak’s house, Ash runs into Gary, and they talk about the upcoming Pokémon League. Professor Oak says they should learn from each other rather than competing, and that teamwork pays off when Team Rocket attacks!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash and his mother take a vacation to Seafoam Island. While he's there, he's looking for the programmer of the Pokédex to learn about the peculiar evolution of SlowPoké. Team Rocket's bumbling brings to light this surprising mystery!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: When Ash wipes out during a surfing attempt, Victor rescues him and tells him about Humungadunga, a giant wave that sweeps through every 20 years. Can Ash and Pikachu give Victor the confidence he needs to catch the giant wave?

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Back in Pallet Town, Ash, and friends meet Florinda, who works at the local nursery. Her Gloom won't evolve, so she’s afraid she's not training it properly. When Team Rocket attacks, everyone sees just how strong a Pokémon can become!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Everyone wants to become the star when a famous director arrives in town! Ash, Brock, Misty, and Team Rocket are working hard to ensure their Pokémon will be the ones to star opposite Wigglytuff in his movie as he solely desires them.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: When the gang attends the premiere of 'Pokémon in Love,' Meowth looks back on his painful past. He shares the story of his life before Team Rocket, including how he learned to walk and talk like a human.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: With only three weeks left before the Pokémon League competition, Ash, Brock, and Misty head up to Mt. Hideaway to find one of the Elite Four. They're after Pokémon training tips, but they discover a land of overgrown Onix instead!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: While Ash and Brock train for the Pokémon League, they uncover a hidden temple containing Pokémon fossils. Misty remembers a hidden city where people built temples to honor Pokémon. Could they have stumbled upon the ancient city of Pokémopolis?

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Our heroes are headed to the Pokémon League tournament at the Indigo Plateau when they meet another Trainer going their way. Unfortunately, all of his Gym badges have been stolen, so he can't compete…unless he takes Ash's badges instead!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: The Pokémon League is about to begin with the ceremonial lighting of the torch, which carries a flame from the Legendary Pokémon Moltres. Of course, Team Rocket tries to steal it!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: It's the first day of the Pokémon League, and when Ash's friends learn his first battle will be held on the Water Field, they're full of advice!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: As the Pokémon League continues, Ash battles on the Rock Field and has a run-in with Team Rocket after they set up a fake Pokémon Center to lure unwary Trainers!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash faces his toughest opponent yet on the Grass Field at the Pokémon League! Are he and his Bulbasaur ready to take on his opponent and her powerful Scyther?

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: When Ash meets a like-minded Trainer named Richie at the Pokémon League, the two boys quickly become close friends—and so do their two Pikachu!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash and Richie will be paired up against each other in the fifth round of the Pokémon League! Can their friendship hold up during this significant battle?

programme.sub-title: I Know What You Did In Drama Class

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Double Talk
programme.desc: Jordan's guest shot on Tamera's radio show turns into a full-time gig, but it may cost them their friendship.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Freaknik
programme.desc: Tia, Tamera, and Diavian all go to FreakNik in Atlanta during Spring Break. Tyreke races to Atlanta hoping to make up with Tia, but it may not be in time to save their relationship.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Before There Was Hip-Hop
programme.desc: An old-time jazz legend fuels dreams of the swing era for the class and dreams of heroics for Jordan.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Let Them Eat Cupcake
programme.desc: A series of mix-ups delays Victor's marriage proposal to Lisa

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Road Less Traveled
programme.desc: Tia considers taking a summer internship with the WNBA in New York. Meanwhile, Lisa worries about her upcoming wedding.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Fly Away Home
programme.desc: Lisa gets cold feet on her wedding day, while Tia and Tamera entertain an anxious crowd waiting for the ceremony.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Meeting
programme.desc: Once separated at birth, identical twins Tia and Tamera, rediscover each other and scheme to live under one roof with their adoptive parents, who have nothing in common but their love for the girls.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: First Dates
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera vie for dates to the big school dance, while Ray and Lisa clash over the rules of the house.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Slumber Party
programme.desc: The boyfriend of one of Tia and Tamera's slumber party guests crashes the bash, while Lisa discovers that her beau is stepping out with another woman.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Cheater Cheater
programme.desc: Tamera cajoles Tia into helping her cheat on a test in order to win Ray's approval.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Wedding Bells & Box Boys
programme.desc: Lisa blames Ray for leading a bridal client astray, while Tia and Tamera see Roger in a whole new light during a double date.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Out Alone
programme.desc: While taking a bus to the movies, Tia and Tamera fight over the disadvantages of being a twin, but when they get lost, they must team up to find their way home.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Pimple
programme.desc: When a pimple pops up just before Tia's big date, Tamera agrees to step in for her -- and finds herself falling for Tia's guy.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Car Trouble
programme.desc: Tamera scratches the fender of her father's prized car, then tries to hide the truth with the help of Tia and Roger.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Birthday
programme.desc: The twins' first birthday together leaves Tamera's best friend feeling left out, and Ray locked in an argument with Lisa over his parenting methods.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Love Strikes
programme.desc: Fearing they'll be separated forever if Ray and Lisa marry other people, Tia and Tamera plot to derail Ray's new romance.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Mothers & Other Strangers
programme.desc: When Lisa's disapproving mother comes for a visit, Ray agrees to pose as her husband.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Concert
programme.desc: After their parents forbid them to attend a rap concert, the twins sneak out of the house to meet their idol anyway.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Hair Today
programme.desc: Tamera's glamorous new look gets her into a snobby clique -- but threatens her relationship with Tia, who's been snubbed by the same crowd.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Get a Job
programme.desc: Bored with their jobs at Ray's limousine company, the twins secretly start working at a burger stand and quickly learn the perils of trying to be in two places at the same time.

programme.desc: Tia and Tamera face "double trouble" when the same guy unknowingly asks them both out on the same date!

programme.desc: Prom night could wind up being a date with disaster when Tia and Tamera's escorts behave badly, and Roger's date towers over him.

programme.desc: Love is in the air when Tamera, Lisa, and Ray all have close encounters of the romantic kind.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Free Billy
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera rescue a pig who's being mistreated by its owner and attempt to secretly stash the "porker" at home.

programme.title: Sister, Sister
programme.desc: While recovering from "twin appendectomies," Tia and Tamera go in search of hospital records that could reveal the identities of their biological parents.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Tattoo
programme.desc: When Lisa forbids Tia to see her boyfriend Michael, Tamera acts as a go-between for the couple -- but finds herself falling for him.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Two for the Road
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera take their sisterly bond a little too far when they collide with each other's cars while learning to drive.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Its A Party Thang
programme.desc: When the "cool crowd" doesn't invite Tia and Tamera to their "happening" party, the twins decide to throw a bash of their own -- which could wind up being a "bust"!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Field Trip
programme.desc: A class field trip to a museum lands the twins in detention, while Roger "borrows" a statue that makes him irresistible to women.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Put to the Test
programme.desc: When Tia and Tamera's SAT scores come in, A-student Tia is stunned to discover that Tamera has pulled a higher score than she has.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Kid in Play
programme.desc: After Tamera wins the lead in the school play, she transforms from a calm high school student into the ultimate "drama diva," who's driving everyone -- particularly Tia -- crazy.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Dream Lover
programme.desc: When Tia confides in Tamera that she dreamed of kissing "Stinky Steve," their boss at Rocket Burger, Steve mistakenly believes she's got "the hots" for him.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Scrambled Eggs
programme.desc: A school project in which Tia and Tamera "marry" Michael and Roger proves to be an eye-opening experience for the twins.

programme.desc: Tia and Tamera have a new "burning passion" -- for cigarettes.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Single White Teenager
programme.desc: When Tia's out sick from school, Tamera befriends a new girl who becomes obsessed with looking and acting like the twins.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: I Do?

programme.desc: The Teletubbies have trouble getting out of bed. The Teletubbies watch Andy Brown and some children feeding swans.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies watch a little boy peel and mash some eggs for an egg mayonnaise sandwich. The Teletubbies laugh when they make a tune whilst eating their Tubby Custard.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky says "Eh-oh". The Teletubbies and some children watch Tamzin Griffin pretend to be the Funny Lady and tell the story of the naughty hat.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a girl talking all about her Dad's lorry. Dipsy goes for a long walk, but the other Teletubbies decide not to join him.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies appear on the hills for a big hug. The run off to watch a lady tell some children an Indian story about a clever fox. The Lion and Bear appear in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are feeling hungry, so they sit down to eat some Tubby Toast, but the Tubby Toaster isn't working. They watch a little girl called Becky make chocolate flake cakes for her friend Mark.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch two girls decorate a pot. Teletubbies love to play, but one by one, their favourite things go missing. Where can they be?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies take turns to stand – on a hill, in front of the house, next to a tree and under a cloud. But each time they stand under the cloud it begins to rain.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children have fun riding their trikes. Po is riding her scooter and the other Teletubbies want to have a go.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are out walking, when their favourite things appear. They leave them to see some children winding up a clockwork robot and watch him walk.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies love to play slide games. They stop when it is time to watch some children go on a train journey. Po loves to ride her scooter, but one day it rolls away and Po chases it all over Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies play a sitting and standing game. They watch some children who are sitting and standing too. Po is riding on her scooter. She hasn’t gone very far when she finds Dipsy’s hat.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy has no time for Tubby Custard, before watching a little girl help feed her baby sister. Po makes some Tubby Custard for Dipsy, but every time she comes to the table, she forgets and eats it all herself.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies and some children learn about the colour blue. In Teletubbyland Po is watching the blue sky when it begins to rain. She finds a blue puddle and is delighted when she sees her reflection.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po is hiding. She comes out to watch a little boy playing crazy golf. In Teletubbyland the Teletubbies are playing a hiding game.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies enjoy a big hug before watching a lady tell a story about four friends. She uses her hands to make actions to tell the story.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Andy Brown and some children watch some badgers. In Teletubbyland, Dipsy sat down to watch. He watched the windmill turning, the rabbits playing, and the trees in the wind.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies love dancing. They watch a group of children perform the Boom Boom Dance, to some drum music.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance in and out of the trees. The Teletubbies watch Matthew, James and Mark feeding their chickens. There's toast all over Teletubbyland when the Tubby Toaster makes too much toast.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A balloon appears in Teletubbyland; it floats in the air. The Teletubbies follow the balloon all over Teletubbyland, but they can't catch it.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies watch some children build towers of different shapes and sizes. Laa-Laa and Dipsy play an up and down game. They take turns to sit down and stand up, then they go up and down the slide.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance round the Dome, but stop to watch Zoe collect some things in her Treasure Box for her sister Sophie. In Teletubbyland, Dipsy hears magical music and sees a cloud.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children go for a ride on a Sea Tractor. Po goes for a ride on her scooter, while she’s riding she sees Laa-Laa’s ball, Dipsy’s hat and Tinky Winky’s bag. They’ve left them all behind!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies play a bumping bottoms game. They stop to watch some children play some ball games. Laa-Laa’s ball goes in a puddle, she tries to get it back but can’t reach it.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children go on a boat trip to see some seals. Tinky Winky is wondering what everyone is doing.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are very, very happy. They watch two boys make Chinese Lanterns with their uncle. In Teletubbyland, it is time for Tubby Toast, but where has Laa-Laa's toast gone?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Andy Brown and some two children look for fox cubs. Tinky Winky falls asleep in bed, Dipsy falls asleep at the table, Laa-Laa falls asleep on the slide and Po falls asleep on the scooter.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Teletubbies love dancing, but they love children even more. So everything stops when they watch some children painting in a most unusual way. Back at the house, a trail leads Tinky Winky all around Teletubbyland to find out.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy and Laa-Laa are out for a walk and Po is riding her scooter, but where is Tinky Winky? When the other Tubbies see him they chase him all over Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The farmyard boys feed their cows and calves and watch them grazing in a field. Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa eat their Tubby Toast at the table, and Po eats her toast in her bed.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children playing with torches. In Teletubbyland, Po chases Tinky Winky, and Po catches him.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises & makes lovely roaring sounds, Dipsy & Laa-Laa laugh and run away. Some children pretend to be different animals and guess what they are. Tinky Winky hears a big roar and runs away.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po pops up between Dipsy and Tinky Winky. They join Laa-Laa to watch two girls go for a tram ride around San Francisco. Laa-Laa and Po go for a walk. Laa-Laa goes the quick way and Po goes the long way.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies pop up to say hello before they gather round to watch some children learn about the number nine. Po and Tinky Winky count nine flowers that appear in Teletubbyland and then run off to fin Dipsy and Laa-Laa and dance the Bump dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies appear one at a time to say ‘Eh-oh’. They watch some children playing with bubbles. It’s time for Tubby Custard. The custard machine makes a funny noise, and a tubby custard bubble comes out followed by more.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch Daniel and Stephanie make big round pancakes. They are having a round and round day. They run round and round in Teletubbyland, but they stop to see the dancing bear in the Carousel, then there's time for one more dance.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Murder Of Stephen Cameron
programme.desc: Gripping documentary examining the fatal stabbing of 21-year-old motorist Stephen Cameron during a motorway altercation in 1996.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Garry Newlove
programme.desc: Garry Newlove’s wife Helen talks about the events on the night of his murder and the days afterwards, as well as her current work as a peer in the House of Lords combatting anti-social behaviour.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Peter Sutcliffe
programme.desc: Arguably Britain's most infamous serial killer, Peter Sutcliffe became known as The Yorkshire Ripper when he began murdering women in Yorkshire during the 1970s.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Christopher Foster
programme.desc: Failed businessman Christopher Foster killed his wife and daughter before burning their mansion in Shropshire to the ground in August 2008.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Marchioness Disaster
programme.desc: The story of the Marchioness; a pleasure boat that collided with dredger Bowbelle and sank within minutes resulting in the loss of 51 lives.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Clare Wood
programme.desc: In April 2007, Clare Wood met George Appleton on a dating website and they embarked on a relationship together. Unbeknownst to her, Appleton was a criminal with a severe history of violence against women.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Suzy Lamplugh
programme.desc: A look at the case of estate agent Suzy Lamplugh, who showed a client around a house in Fulham in July 1986 and was never seen again. Dermot Murnaghan hosts.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Wrongly Released
programme.desc: This special episode shines a light on criminals who have been brought to justice for one crime only to be released to commit further terrible acts.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rochdale Groomers
programme.desc: Rochdale Groomers: Documentary examining the sex abuse scandal which occurred in the North of England and appeared to go undetected for quite some time.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Joanne Dennehy
programme.desc: Dermot Murnaghan returns to uncover the truth behind the horrific crimes of Peterborough serial killer Joanna Dennehy.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Peter Tobin
programme.desc: Peter Tobin is one of the most notorious serial killers in the UK. From 1991–2006 he raped and murdered three young girls; Dinah McNicol, Vicky Hamilton and Angelika Kluk. Yet even prior to these horrific murders he had a history of violence.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: John Sweeney
programme.desc: John Sweeney brutally murdered two of his girlfriends, dismembered their bodies and dumped them in canals. In a macabre twist, he hid clues in his abstract paintings, and five other girlfriends of his are currently still missing.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Manchester
programme.desc: The series returns to take a closer look at the most horrific crimes ever committed on British soil, starting with the May 2017 Manchester Arena bombing.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Ann Maguire
programme.desc: The tragic story of Ann Maguire, who was stabbed to death by a teenage pupil while teaching a Spanish lesson at Corpus Christi Catholic College in Leeds.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Gemma McCluskie
programme.desc: The harrowing story of EastEnders actress Gemma McCluskie, who was murdered by her older brother in the flat they shared in east London in 2012.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stepping Hill
programme.desc: An inside look at the series of mysterious deaths that occurred at Stepping Hill Hospital in Stockport, Greater Manchester, in 2011.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Steven Wright
programme.desc: A closer look at the crimes of Steve Wright, who was dubbed 'The Suffolk Strangler' after murdering five women who worked as prostitutes in Ipswich in 2006.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephen Port
programme.desc: A chilling look at the crimes of Stephen Port, the east London serial killer who poisoned four young men with lethal doses of a date rape drug.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephanie Slater
programme.desc: Estate agent Stephanie Slater reveals the story of her abduction, rape and incarceration, which occurred in 1992 whilst showing a man around a house.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Bad Timing
programme.desc: Joan begins to question her relationship with Sean when he gives her a power drill for Valentine's and doesn't hear from him for three days.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Old Dog
programme.desc: Joan decides it is time to give in on her three-month rule and she is finally going to have sex with her boyfriend Sean.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Declaration of Lynndependence
programme.desc: Joan and Toni decide that Lynn needs to learn to be independent. They give her a week to find a job, then she is on her own.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Friends, Colleagues, Brothers
programme.desc: Joan discovers that being the only black woman at her firm has many disadvantages and she is constantly ignored and overlooked.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Diss-regard
programme.desc: Maya checks into the hospital for her fibroid surgery and Joan has trouble managing her time between her boyfriend and her friends.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Burning Vagina Monologues
programme.desc: Joan is stressing because Sean hasn't told her that he loves her.

programme.title: Girlfriends: A Kiss Before Lying
programme.desc: Sean notices that Joan always flirts with Davis. He gets jealous and tells Joan that she can never see Davis again or go back to 847.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Loose Lips Sink Relationships
programme.desc: Joan opens her mouth and tells Greg about Toni and Clay.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Jamaic-Up?
programme.desc: Joan takes the girls to the Sinbad Music Festival to help ease Toni to get over her breakup with Greg.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Fallout
programme.desc: The ladies return home from their very eventful trip to Jamaica where Joan unintentionally ruined Toni's relationship.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Just Say No
programme.desc: Joan continues to suffer in her troubled friendship with Toni and her new relationship with her therapist, Dr. Bales.

programme.title: Girlfriends: A Full Court Conspiracy
programme.desc: Torn between her two worlds, Joan struggles with her professional duty while at the same time dealing with the guilt that she is not being true to herself or her beliefs.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Un-Treatable
programme.desc: Maya buys a dress that is too expensive knowing it might cause problems with her husband, Darnell. Joan treats herself to a trip to New York to visit her boyfriend, realizing the distance between the two of them is a problem.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Buh-Bye
programme.desc: Joan ends the friendship between her and Toni after finding out that Toni has been discussing her personal life with co-workers behind her back. Meanwhile, Lynn is trying to cope with her new living situation with Vosco, and his lack of a job.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Willie or Won’t He
programme.desc: Yvonne is concerned that William is never going to marry her because they have not set a date for the wedding. When Yvonne asks Joan for advice, Joan tries her best to stay out of it but ends up telling Yvonne to move in with William first to try it out.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Joan’s Birthday Suit
programme.desc: For her thirtieth birthday, Joan finds that she is happy with who she is, yet finds that she’s not so happy that her father is a nudist.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Trick or Truth?
programme.desc: When Toni loses her friend Joan, her man, and her gold card, it’s up to Maya and Lynn to help her get her life back together.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Maya Takes a Stan
programme.desc: Due to marital tensions at home, an emotionally fragile Maya finds herself falling into a fantasy affair with a handsome and empathetic new friend, Stan.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Mom’s the Word
programme.desc: Toni meets a great man but is dumped after she’s a bad influence on his eight-year-old daughter. Joan dates a movie star but is dumped when he begins to think she’s stalking him.

programme.title: Girlfriends: You Better Watch Out
programme.desc: Maya refuses to end her relationship with Stan, despite her girlfriends' repeated warnings to do so.

programme.title: Girlfriends: I Have a Dream House
programme.desc: As a gesture of his love, William buys a beautiful house for his fiance, Yvonne.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Sister, Sistah
programme.desc: Lynn's Caucasian sister, Tanya, travels to LA to help Lynn celebrate her Gotcha Day: the day when Lynn's childhood adoption to her Caucasian family became legal.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Willie or Won’t He II: The Last Chapter
programme.desc: The week of William and Yvonne's wedding is finally here and all goes smoothly until Yvonne discovers William never told his mother about Yvonne...ever.
programme.sub-title: Willie or Won’t He II: The Last Chapter

programme.title: Girlfriends: Can’t Stan Ya
programme.desc: After a horrid kiss, Maya finally realizes she must end her relationship with Stan. Afterward, however, Stan deviously becomes good friends with Maya’s husband, just to make Maya squirm as he says he may or may not tell her husband about them.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Take Me Out After the Ball Game
programme.desc: As the company softball season kicks off, Joan falls for her new teammate: a young new employee from the mailroom; however, things go sour when she publicly dismisses their relationship due to their age difference.
programme.sub-title: Take Me Out After the Ball Game

programme.title: Girlfriends: Child’s in Charge
programme.desc: Toni decides to make something of her life by opening her own exclusive real estate agency after she sells a four-point-two million-dollar property.

programme.title: Girlfriends: X Does Not Mark the Spot
programme.desc: Joan becomes more adventurous with her boyfriend Chris, but inadvertently takes Ecstasy at a club with him and has a bad trip that puts her into the hospital.
programme.sub-title: X Does Not Mark the Spot

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Taming of the Realtess
programme.desc: Toni is frantic as the grand opening of her real estate agency is less than a week away.

programme.title: Girlfriends: My Mother, Myself
programme.desc: Just before Mother’s Day, Maya is disturbed when her mother announces she’s selling the house and moving to San Bernardino with her boyfriend. Toni has issues when her mother shows up unannounced for Mother’s Day and continually embarrasses her.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Just Dessert
programme.desc: Unable to work out living with Joan, Lynn moves in with William and the two hit it off in more ways than one; becoming casual, unattached sex buddies.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Into the Woods
programme.desc: Joan dumps Chris when she learns her ex-boyfriend, Sean, is moving back to town; but is crushed when Sean lies about being engaged just to avoid being in a relationship with her again.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Getting Our Act Together
programme.desc: Maya dreams of reuniting with Darnell, Lynn becomes a bartender and William tries to fast-track his partnership at the law firm.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Handling Baggage
programme.desc: Maya struggles to save her marriage.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Coming to Terms
programme.desc: Eager to meet new men, Joan throws a "Bring Your Ex" party

programme.title: Girlfriends: Secrets and Eyes
programme.desc: Toni wrestles with growing older, and William makes a love connection.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Invasion of the Golddigger
programme.desc: Joan is not crazy about William’s new girlfriend, while Toni’s plastic surgeon comes up short on the dating front.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Don't Leave Me a Loan
programme.desc: While Maya tries to hide her marital woes from her mother, Lynn faces a financial reckoning with her parents

programme.title: Girlfriends: Star Craving Mad
programme.desc: Joan meets a movie star who isn’t as deep as he seems, and William’s sister makes a surprising request.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Blinded by the Lights
programme.desc: Joan discovers that dating a screen star isn’t as glamorous as it seems.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Mommy Returns
programme.desc: Lynn’s long-lost birth mother resurfaces after 30 years

programme.title: What We Know With Max Foster
programme.desc: Max Foster brings a definitive round-up of the day's top stories, giving you the urgent need-to-know context and diving deeper into the questions sparking debate around the world.

programme.title: Quest Means Business
programme.desc: Richard Quest presents a round-up of the day's global business news with a focus on international business and market trends.

programme.title: Quest's World of Wonder
programme.desc: Larger-than-life characters take us on a journey with Richard Quest to experience cities in a way tourists can't.

programme.title: The Lead With Jake Tapper
programme.desc: Covering all the day's top stories around the country and the globe, from politics to money, sports to popular culture.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: The Brief With Jim Sciutto
programme.desc: The best of CNN's reporting from correspondents across the world, to guide global audiences through the fast-paced news cycle with insight, analysis and hard-hitting interviews.

programme.title: Erin Burnett OutFront
programme.desc: Anchor Erin Burnett presents information beyond the regular headlines by providing objective reports on top issues along with compelling interviews, lively debates and in-depth analysis of situations.

programme.title: Anderson Cooper 360
programme.desc: Anderson Cooper brings his prickly but often witty perspective to current events and personalities in the news. He is joined by a series of guests that frequently include political and legal analysts.

programme.title: The Source With Kaitlan Collins
programme.desc: Kaitlan Collins chases the facts, asks the tough questions and connects with her sources on prime time to break down the day's biggest news stories.

programme.title: CNN NewsNight With Abby Phillip
programme.desc: Abby Phillip delivers a smart, sharp approach to the day's biggest stories.

programme.title: Laura Coates Live
programme.desc: Laura Coates discusses the daily intersections of politics, pop culture and the law.

programme.title: The Story Is With Elex Michaelson
programme.desc: Elex Michaelson tackles the breaking stories making headlines with sharp analysis, fresh perspective, and a dose of humour.

programme.title: The Story Is With Elex Michaelson
programme.desc: Elex Michaelson tackles the breaking stories making headlines with sharp analysis, fresh perspective, and a dose of humour.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom
programme.desc: A rotating cast of anchors and contributors present daily headlines and breaking stories live from CNN headquarters in Atlanta.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: CNN Headline Express
programme.desc: The biggest headlines and most buzzworthy stories with live updates throughout the day.

programme.title: CNN News Central
programme.desc: The latest news from around the world live from CNN's immersive news hub with John Berman, Kate Bolduan and Sara Sidner.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team helps a group of overworked and underpaid farm workers institute a union but their boss has other ideas.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team decide to help a family whose livelihood as fishermen seems doomed due to an extortionist.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team joins forces with three handicapped Vietnam veterans and Amanda Huston, to help keep one hungry and insidious Frank Gaines off their land and out of "Deadman," their town. Gaines, on the other hand, has plans to eliminate them altogether.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The demolition of an old warehouse becomes a war zone with the A-Team when a dangerous mob figure does not want old evidence excavated from the ruins.1

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Face becomes a candidate for Sheriff in a small Wyoming town in hopes of exposing its incumbent, a crooked law enforcement officer.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Sam Yeng and his restaurant, the Golden Pagoda, risk ruination at the hands of a powerful Chinese group due to protection money owed.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team is hired to prove the innocence of a man convicted of smuggling cocaine in South America.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Undercover reporter Tawnia Baker contacts the A-Team with a special request.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team members are hired to expose a murderer who is selling weapons from an Army base to the highest bidder.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: When a busload of elderly people are robbed by a desert gang, the A-Team is hired to retrieve their possessions.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team must break up a car theft ring before B.A.'s van is converted into a million parts.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team matches wits with a terrorist group but the case takes unexpected turns.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: B.A., Face and Murdock are captured by terrorists and held in a dairy farm prison, leaving Tawnia and Hannibal to devise a rescue mission.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team members are hired to help a peaceful group known as the Society of Men remain in their community.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: It looks like the A-Team's time has run out when Lt. Decker and his men have them surrounded and Murdock is suffering from a gunshot wound which requires immediate medical attention.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The team flies to Miami to wage war against gangsters threatening to take over a resort hotel owned by two sisters.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: A river pirate captures a man as he travels into uncharted Amazon River territory.
programme.sub-title: The Bend In The River, Pt 1

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The A-Team hopes to rescue Tawnia's fiance who has been captured by a river pirate in the Brazilian rainforest.

programme.desc: Graham is joined by Kit Harington, Matt LeBlanc, Rebel Wilson and Mumford and Sons.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Among the guests on Graham's sofa: everyone's favourite hot vicar from 'Fleabag' Andrew Scott, Stephen Fry on tour with 'Mythos: A Trilogy', singer-turned-actor Paloma Faith, and top comic Lee Mack. With music from Bastille, who perform 'Joy'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Among the guests on Graham's sofa tonight are Rosamund Pike starring in the movie 'A United Kingdom', top comic Michael McIntyre, theatrical Andrew Lloyd Webber, talking about his musical 'School of Rock', and chat and music from Coldplay.
programme.sub-title: Rosamund Pike, Andrew Lloyd Webber, Michael McIntyre and Coldplay

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: A compilation of highlights from the 21st series, featuring Morgan Freeman, Sir Michael Caine, Diane Keaton, Warren Beatty, Goldie Hawn, Sir Patrick Stewart, Nicole Kidman, Mark Wahlberg, Salma Hayek, Zac Efron, Rachel Weisz, and more.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Together on Graham's sofa are Oscar-winner Anne Hathaway, actresses Rebel Wilson and Jodie Comer, and actor Daniel Radcliffe. With music from Mabel, who performs her single 'Don't Call Me Up'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Among the guests on Graham's sofa are British talent Tom Hiddleston, Oscar-nominated John Malkovich, UK comedian and author Sara Pascoe and actor Samuel L Jackson, with music from Chvrches.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Together on Graham's sofa are 'X-Men' star James McAvoy with his co-star Sarah Paulson, iconic star of 'Withnail and I' Richard E Grant, and Steve Coogan and John C Reilly. Also featuring music from Westlife.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Isabel
programme.desc: Maiya experiences dimensional phasing. Augur and Liam believe they need to bring Isabel and Maiya together to let the universe merge them. Jonathan Doors announces his presidential candidacy and meets with Zo'or.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict
programme.desc: An escaped Jiridian takes Lili hostage. Their shuttle is sucked on to a Kimera ship where they, with Liam, have to go through an obstacle course to get to the core to exit.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict
programme.desc: Using Jiridian technology, scientists create a physical bio surrogates where a human consciousness can be transferred. When a surrogate escapes, they must find it before it uses its fighting and weapons knowledge.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Second Chances
programme.desc: A Taelon program offers a transformation into a younger, healthier version of oneself. Liam fears that something more dangerous is also happening. Lili is worried that Doors' run for the presidency is negatively affecting the resistance.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Payback
programme.desc: A mysterious death by poisoning creates questions about the safety of portal technology. When a Taelon is killed in the same manner, the investigation leads to a very smart scientist that even the Resistance was scared to work with.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Friendly Fire
programme.desc: Paul Chandler plots an attack on the Taelon Embassy in DC with the ship he stole from Lili. He begs her to join him in beginning an all out war.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Volunteers
programme.desc: A group of Resistance capture a volunteer from a Taelon program for youth. Liam decides they may learn valuable information from her. They also decide to send in one of their own as a volunteer to see what the program is teaching.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Bliss
programme.desc: Lili is given a psychotropic drug called "bliss" via a Taelon device. They discover it is widespread. With an instantly strong addictive property, the addicts are dying within 48 hours. The investigation leads Liam and Sandoval to Ireland.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Highjacked
programme.desc: With a reporter on board, the Taelon mother ship is highjacked into interdimensional space. Liam is the prime suspect, causing an even bigger rift between Da'an and Zo'or. As the ship floats, lost in space, they must find the real culprit.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Defector
programme.desc: A shuttle crashes on a beach after being pursued by a second Taelon piloted shuttle. When Liam locates the survivor (a Taelon called Belleye), he claims to want to help the Resistance take back Earth from the Taelons.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Heroes and Heartbreak
programme.desc: Augur is recruited by a Taelon weapons project, but the others are worried about his involvement. When the project is launched earlier than originally planned to fight a Jiridian fleet, the Earth is in danger.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Message in a Bottle
programme.desc: A Jiridian probe headed for Earth is shot down by the Taelon Embassy and crashes outside of D.C. Liam recovers the probe, but he, Augur, and Julia are captured by another underground resistance movement led by the real Colonel Liam Kincaid.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Crossfire
programme.desc: As the election draws near, Liam believes Zo'or is trying to have Jonathan Doors murdered. The Resistance worries that an attack is imminent at the final debate. Julia mobilizes a group to protect Doors against Liam's wishes.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Crackdown
programme.desc: An unknown woman saves Augur and Liam from the raids on the Resistance. Lili sabotages the mother ship, but Zo'or stops its total destruction. Lili is reportedly dead. The investigation on the President's shooter continues.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Vanished
programme.desc: Some who were detained during the Resistance sweep have not reappeared after their release. Lili is taken to a lab run by Sandoval and implanted. Renee tries to help Liam find the missing people.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Emancipation
programme.desc: A group posing as volunteers attacks using a stolen skrill prototype that does not require an implant to control. It is discovered that the skrill queen is also missing. The investigation leads to a resistance group called Black Wednesday.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Déjà Vu
programme.desc: A joint Doors International/Taelon venture creates a machine that allows people to vividly relive a memory, but Liam suspects a back alley version of the device is leading to the death of some users.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Once and Future World
programme.desc: Artifacts believed to be Taelon are found in South America. Renee and Liam try to beat Sandoval to the source to determine the importance of the found pieces.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Thicker than Blood
programme.desc: An old business partner of Augur's is hired by Da'an to get information about the Resistance, resulting in the death of many members. Meanwhile, Sandoval finds out he has a life threatening blood disorder.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: A Little Bit of Heaven
programme.desc: Liam and Renee investigate a dead pregnant woman missing her fetus. They discover a town that Da'an helped after a nuclear meltdown and a suspicious brother and sister who were part of an early experiment to combine human and Taelon DNA.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Pad'ar
programme.desc: Pad'ar is a fierce battle game where spectators can connect via a "jack patch" that Liam suspects is causing neurological damage to users. When a Pad'ar warrior dies, it raises even more questions about the safety.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Thomas Plant and Paul Laidlaw spend their final day of antique adventuring heading from Tetsworth to the final auction showdown in Pewsey.
programme.sub-title: Thomas Plant and Paul Laidlaw, Day 5

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: The girls pose for their first official Top Model photoshoot but quickly learn they are only allowed to wear one article of clothing.

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: The girls receive a surprise visit from cycle 3 contestant Toccara Jones, who puts their fashion knowledge to the test. This week's photoshoot is dance-inspired and forces the girls to pose while demonstrating their fancy footwork.

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: The girls learn they will be portraying vampires in a tub of blood in this week's photoshoot challenge. Meanwhile, back at their NYC loft, screaming and yelling escalates to an all-time high between two of the girls.

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: This week the girls pose with comedian Ross Mathews (The Tonight Show and Chelsea Lately) before heading to NYC's garment district to be completely transformed in faux fashion wardrobe.

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: This week's photo challenge finds the girls striking a pose while on board a New York City Subway car. While at panel, judge André Leon Talley offers a blunt critique of one model's outfit.

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: Guest judge Whitney Port drops by the loft to surprise the girls before they put their personalities to the test during a runway challenge. This week's photoshoot finds the girls posing with big hair.

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: The final six girls arrive in New Zealand where they learn they will participate in go-sees with six designers. This week's photoshoot finds them all modeling the same dress with a special guest, a sheep!

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: Special guest judge and Lord of the Rings actress Sarah McLeod welcome the girls to the Hobbiton in New Zealand, where the girls do a quick five-frame photo shoot. During this week's photoshoot, Tyra goes behind the camera with the final five models.

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: The final four learn that at the end of tonight's episode only two will remain. This week's shoot finds them in Queenstown, New Zealand where they must be ugly-pretty. In the finale, America's Next Top Model is chosen.
programme.sub-title: Ugly-Pretty Woman / America's Next Top Model Is...

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: Marshmallows, catfights, cockroaches and catch phrases are revealed in the never-before-seen footage from Cycle 14.

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: Thirty-three semi-finalists compete for a chance to be Tyra's friend but only 13 will make it into the Top Model loft in New York City. The lucky finalists are shocked when special guest Perez Hilton reveals their first challenge is to undergo makeovers!
programme.sub-title: Be My Friend, Tyra! / Make Over Madness

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: The girls pose for their first official Top Model photoshoot but quickly learn they are only allowed to wear one article of clothing.

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: The girls receive a surprise visit from cycle 3 contestant Toccara Jones, who puts their fashion knowledge to the test. This week's photoshoot is dance-inspired and forces the girls to pose while demonstrating their fancy footwork.

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: The girls learn they will be portraying vampires in a tub of blood in this week's photoshoot challenge. Meanwhile, back at their NYC loft, screaming and yelling escalates to an all-time high between two of the girls.

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: This week the girls pose with comedian Ross Mathews (The Tonight Show and Chelsea Lately) before heading to NYC's garment district to be completely transformed in faux fashion wardrobe.

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: This week's photo challenge finds the girls striking a pose while on board a New York City Subway car. While at panel, judge André Leon Talley offers a blunt critique of one model's outfit.

programme.title: America's Next Top Model
programme.desc: Guest judge Whitney Port drops by the loft to surprise the girls before they put their personalities to the test during a runway challenge. This week's photoshoot finds the girls posing with big hair.
